source.title: Does assimilation into schemas involve systems or cellular consolidation? It is not just time. (Companion to Tse et al 2007 Science 316:76-82)

confidence_rationale: Argues for multi-scale hippocampal organisation -- coarse schema and fine episode levels. Strong behavioural and lesion-timing evidence that schemas at the environment-plus-task level dramatically accelerate consolidation, suggesting biology uses a coarser-than-place-cell-field unit for high-impact organisation. Mapping to substrate granularity choice is interpretive.
failure_signatures: Schema-level granularity (coarse, environment-plus-task) is what biology uses for consolidation gating. A substrate that defaults to place-cell-field grain will miss the schema-level V_s computation.
Single-scale anchor sets cannot represent the hierarchical schema-contains-episode structure that the Morris paradigm relies on.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Rats with a pre-trained spatial schema (an event arena with stable food-location associations) encode new paired associates in a single trial; these schema-fitted memories survive hippocampal lesions much sooner than non-schema-based memories.
mapping.ree_translation: Hippocampal organisation is multi-scale: a coarse schema layer (environment-plus-task, gates consolidation) and a fine episode/association layer (slots specific items into schema). MECH-269 anchors are most naturally at the schema scale; the substrate should organise anchors hierarchically rather than as a flat list. V_s should be computed at both scales: schema-V_s (does the framework still apply?) and episode-V_s (does this association still hold?).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Morris paradigm is stylised; schema is operationalised as broad framework of stable associations, not formally defined. Whether competing schemas for the same arena can coexist (the dual-trace question at the schema level) is not tested.
mapping.source_context: Behavioural and lesion experiments in rats trained on the event arena paired-associate task; companion theoretical defence of schema interpretation.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality but the multi-scale architectural recommendation is interpretive. Stylised paradigm constrains transfer; the schema scale is the right level for V_s gating but the specific implementation in the substrate plan needs the hierarchy to be designed explicitly.
